title: 9 1 Periodicity
body:
TOPIC 9 : FOURIER SERIES I 9.1 PERIODICITY

• A function f is said to have a period of T if f (x + T ) = f (x) for all x. 2π • sin(x) and cos(x) have period 2π; sin(nx) and cos(nx) have period . n • A function f is odd if f (−x) = −f (x) for all x. Sine is an example of an odd function.

• A function f is even if f (−x) = f (x) for all x. Cosine is an example of an even function.

Odd × Odd = Even, Even × Even = Even, Odd × Even = Odd.

Odd ± Odd = Odd, Even ± Even = Even, Odd ± Even = Neither. Z a Z a Z a Odd dx = 0, Even dx = 2 Even dx. −a −a 0

This lecture will prepare you for the theory of Fourier series by investigating various issues and definitions surrounding the concept of periodicity. It is quite common for physical systems to have a strong cyclical or periodic behaviour. For example consider the motion of a piston or the oscillation of a audio speaker. Fourier series provide us with the tools to analyse these systems effectively by transforming periodic signals into series of sines and cosines, which are of course the fundamental periodic objects in mathematics.

Definition: A function f is said to be periodic if f (t + T ) = f (t) for some T . This means that the function repeats itself every T units. The period of the function is the smallest value of T that satisfies the above equation.

Example 4 Identify each of the following functions as odd, even or neither:

i) f (x) = cos(3x) ii) f (x) = x cos(x) iii) f (x) = sin2 (x) iv) Prove your answers from the definition.

Knowing that a function is odd or even can make it easier to calculate integrals involving that function. It is important to remember that following results only apply when the integral is over an interval [−a, a].

Claim: The integral of any odd function over the interval [−a, a] is exactly zero. Z a Odd dx = 0. −a

Proof:

Claim: The integral of any even function over the interval [−a, a] is twice the integral over the interval [0, a]. Z a Z a Even dx = 2 Even dx. −a 0

Proof:

Example 6 Prove that the derivative of an even function is an odd function.

PERIODIC EXTENSION OF A FUNCTION

If we are given a function f (t) defined over some interval a ≤ t ≤ b, then we can easily make it into a periodic function by repeating it over and over. This is called the periodic extension of a function. However, it is often the case that we don’t just want a periodic function, but rather a periodic function that has odd or even symmetry. We achieve this by first making f (t) an odd or even function by appropriately flipping it in the horizontal or vertical directions, and then repeating this basic odd or even tile. This is called the odd or even periodic extension of a function.  2t, 0 ≤ t < 1; Example 7 Suppose that f (t) = 2, 1 ≤ t < 2.

i) Sketch f over 0 ≤ t ≤ 2. ii) Sketch g, the periodic extension of f over −8 ≤ t ≤ 8. What is the period of g? Evaluate g(8.3). iii) Sketch h, the even periodic extension of f over −8 ≤ t ≤ 8. What is the period of h? iv) Sketch j, the odd periodic extension of f over −8 ≤ t ≤ 8. What is the period of j?
